findings.issue: TDD Anchor section is absent. For an audit task (no code produced), this is expected and correct per template (non-code tasks should omit TDD Anchor).
findings.fix: No fix required — documented here as explicit acknowledgement, not a defect.

findings.issue: Verification Steps contains only '### Automated' subsection with narrative text explaining that the task does not run tests. No Smoke or User subsections.
findings.fix: Acceptable for an audit task — the audit produces a JSON report and the verification is JSON schema validity. Consider adding an explicit command like 'jq . work/phase-1-foundation/logs/audit/code-review.json' to align with Task 13's stricter style.

findings.issue: The 'Dependencies' subsection mixes task dependencies with optional context (between-task review reports). Minor — clarity rather than correctness.
findings.fix: Optional: split into 'Hard dependencies' (Task 10) and 'Optional context' (between-task reviews).

findings.issue: Manifest & config / App sources / Core-runtime sources are listed as bold-labelled bullet groups with inline code spans (e.g. 'app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml'), not as markdown links. Template prescribes markdown-link form '[name](path)'.
findings.fix: Convert code-span paths to markdown links (or keep as-is with a 'Code files (read-only):' header like Task 11 does). Not blocking — paths are still machine-identifiable — but inconsistent with template and with Task 11's style in the same batch.

findings.issue: Verification Steps has only '### Automated' with plain bullet checks about the output JSON file. No explicit commands (jq/test -f) like Task 13 provides.
findings.fix: Optional: add concrete commands — 'jq . work/phase-1-foundation/logs/audit/security-review.json' and 'jq .tacVerification ...' — to match Task 13's style.

findings.issue: AC list is long (11 items) and some items overlap with scope/process constraints rather than user-visible outcomes ('Новые тесты в ходе аудита не пишутся', 'Scope-lock подтверждён'). These are process rules, not testable acceptance criteria on the artefact.
findings.fix: Optional: move process-rules to the Description/What to do and keep AC focused on artefact properties (file exists, schema valid, specific metrics set).

findings.issue: Dependencies subsection lists 'Task 11 (Deep polish / security)', 'Task 12 (если существует в tech-spec как полирование)', 'Task 13 (финальная сборка / APK)' — but in this feature Task 11/12/13 are the three Audit Wave tasks (Code/Security/Test Audit) and the APK is produced by the build command inside Task 14 itself, not by a prior task. Mismatches frontmatter depends_on [11,12,13] semantics and tech-spec reality.
findings.fix: Rewrite Dependencies subsection to reflect the Audit Wave: 'Task 11 (Code Audit), Task 12 (Security Audit), Task 13 (Test Audit) — все три должны завершиться до запуска QA. APK собирается внутри этой задачи через ./gradlew build.'

findings.issue: 'app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk — артефакт сборки (создаётся в Task 13)' — Task 13 is Test Audit in this feature, not a build task. APK is produced by Task 14's own gradle build.
findings.fix: Correct the parenthetical to 'создаётся ./gradlew build внутри этой задачи' or drop the file from Context Files (it is an output of this task, not a read input).

findings.issue: Paths to project-knowledge files use mixed formats: '[project.md](../../../.claude/skills/project-knowledge/project.md)' but patterns/architecture files elsewhere in the feature use '.../references/project.md'. Inconsistent with Tasks 11/12/13 which point to '.../references/project.md'.
findings.fix: Verify actual location of project-knowledge reference files and unify the path form across batch (either .claude/skills/project-knowledge/*.md or .claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/*.md).

findings.issue: AC references 'TAC-16 (AC-14) выполнен' and lists 'все TAC-1 … TAC-15 проверены' — but TAC list in Details only defines TAC-1..TAC-15 plus a TAC-16 populated after user measurements. Minor labelling inconsistency.
findings.fix: Align AC to explicitly state TAC-1..TAC-15 are agent-automated and TAC-16 is user-assisted measurement protocol.

notes.frontmatter: All four tasks have valid frontmatter: status=planned, depends_on arrays with existing task numbers (Tasks 11-13 depend on [10], Task 14 on [11,12,13]), wave numbers (5 for audits, 6 for QA) are strictly increasing relative to dependencies, skills arrays correctly single-element ([code-reviewing], [security-auditor], [test-master], [pre-deploy-qa]), verify=[] (valid array), reviewers=[] (valid — these are self-verifying audit/QA tasks). No extra fields.
notes.structure: All mandatory sections present in correct order (Title, Required Skills, Description, What to do, Acceptance Criteria, Context Files, Verification Steps, Details, Reviewers, Post-completion). TDD Anchor correctly omitted in all four (non-code tasks). No template placeholders remain.
notes.internal_consistency: skills frontmatter matches Required Skills in each task. reviewers=[] matches 'Reviewers: none' narrative in each body. Correct.
notes.decomposition_cross_task: Audit Wave (Tasks 11-13) correctly parallelized — distinct reviewers (code-reviewing / security-auditor / test-master), non-overlapping focus (code quality / OWASP / test quality). Task 14 correctly depends on all three. No dependency cycles. No merge candidates (each audit is distinct skill). No split candidates.
notes.carry_forward: Tech-spec Implementation Tasks for Tasks 11-14 are brief but all carry-forward items are present or extended in the task files. Task 11 covers D1-D12 and T1-T11 verification as specified. Task 12 covers OWASP + TAC-13 HF-token removal as specified. Task 13 covers D8 AllowlistLoaderTest audit as specified. Task 14 covers TAC-1..TAC-16 + AC-14 protocol as specified.
notes.resource_sharing: No new shared heavy resources introduced by these tasks (they only read artefacts produced by earlier tasks). N/A for this batch.
